tests.description: The first card ever recorded starts the trail.
tests.key: addToHistory › handles an empty history

tests.description: Newest day first, so the trail reads backwards from today.
tests.key: addToHistory › keeps entries newest-first

tests.description: Re-adding a day replaces it rather than duplicating the row.
tests.key: addToHistory › never keeps two entries for the same day

tests.description: An out-of-order arrival still sorts into place.
tests.key: addToHistory › sorts an older entry into position

tests.description: Corrupt or hand-edited storage must not crash the screen.
tests.key: cardByIndex › falls back to the first card for an out-of-range index

tests.description: The stored draw keeps an arcana index; it must resolve back to its card.
tests.key: cardByIndex › finds the card with the given arcana number

tests.description: Whatever the date, the result is always a real card in the deck.
tests.key: cardForDate › always returns a card from the deck

tests.description: The whole promise of the daily card: a date maps to one card, forever.
tests.key: cardForDate › is deterministic for a given date

tests.description: Different days should not all land on the same card.
tests.key: cardForDate › spreads different dates across different cards

tests.description: A negative index must not wrap round to the end of the deck.
tests.key: cardFromParam › returns null for a negative index

tests.description: Past the end of the deck there is no card to show.
tests.key: cardFromParam › returns null for an index beyond the last card

tests.description: Junk in the URL should land you on the spread, not an error.
tests.key: cardFromParam › returns null for non-integer input

tests.description: The ordinary case: no deep link, so the screen opens the spread.
tests.key: cardFromParam › returns null when no index is given

tests.description: A valid index names its card.
tests.key: cardFromParam › returns the card at a valid index

tests.description: An unknown filename falls back to the back rather than crashing the render.
tests.key: cardImage › falls back to the card back for an unknown filename

tests.description: Every card in the deck must resolve to its own artwork — a card whose image is missing would silently render as a face-down back while claiming to be face-up.
tests.key: cardImage › resolves artwork for all 22 cards

tests.description: A missing translation falls back to Spanish rather than rendering blank — an untranslated card should still say something.
tests.key: cardText › falls back to Spanish when a translation is empty

tests.description: Every card in the deck must have text in both languages.
tests.key: cardText › has non-empty text for all 22 cards in both languages

tests.description: English reads from the parallel `_en` fields.
tests.key: cardText › returns the English translation when asked for English

tests.description: Spanish is the deck's own language — the field is used as written.
tests.key: cardText › returns the Spanish text unchanged

tests.description: Outer cards must stop compounding, or they end up upside-down.
tests.key: clamp › caps anything further out at one card

tests.description: Inside the range a value is its own answer — the centre card and its immediate neighbours must keep their exact positions.
tests.key: clamp › leaves values within one card either side untouched

tests.description: Spanish lowercases weekdays and months; only the sentence starts capital. CSS `capitalize` would give "Lunes, 20 De Julio", which is why this is done in code.
tests.key: formatDay › capitalizes only the first letter in Spanish

tests.description: The same day, read in English.
tests.key: formatDay › formats a day in English

tests.description: The daily card's date line, in the reader's language.
tests.key: formatDay › formats a day in Spanish

tests.description: A date string is parsed as a local calendar day, so the weekday cannot drift by one across timezones.
tests.key: formatDay › reads the date as a local day, not UTC

tests.description: Single-digit days carry no padding once rendered.
tests.key: formatDay › renders single-digit days without a leading zero

tests.description: First visit ever: a card is drawn for today and stored face-down.
tests.key: loadDaily › creates a new face-down draw when nothing is stored

tests.description: A stale card never opened is simply dropped — it was never really yours.
tests.key: loadDaily › discards a stale draw that was never revealed

tests.description: A stale card that was opened graduates into the trail.
tests.key: loadDaily › moves a revealed stale draw into history

tests.description: Returning the same day gives back the same draw, still open if it was.
tests.key: loadDaily › returns the stored draw when it belongs to today

tests.description: Before any day has rolled over the trail is empty, not undefined.
tests.key: loadHistory › returns an empty list when nothing is stored

tests.description: Otherwise it hands back what was recorded.
tests.key: loadHistory › returns the stored entries

tests.description: The passed-in draw is not mutated.
tests.key: revealToday › leaves the original draw untouched

tests.description: Flipping the card face-up records it, so a reload keeps it open.
tests.key: revealToday › marks the draw revealed and persists it

tests.description: Position must not predict the arcana, or choosing a face-down card would just be picking a number.
tests.key: shuffledDeck › does not always put the same card first

tests.description: The source deck is module-level and shared; shuffling in place would reorder it for the daily card and the gallery too.
tests.key: shuffledDeck › leaves the source deck untouched

tests.description: Two shuffles landing in the same order 22! times over is impossible in practice, so identical results across several draws means it is not actually shuffling.
tests.key: shuffledDeck › produces a different order across repeated draws

tests.description: Every card is dealt exactly once — a spread that dropped or repeated a card would let you choose one that cannot be revealed.
tests.key: shuffledDeck › returns all 22 arcana with no duplicates and no omissions

tests.description: A side card hangs lower, tilts outward and dims — the look the reference spread has.
tests.key: slotSamples › drops, tilts and dims the neighbouring slots at rest

tests.description: Outer slots are already fully tilted and dimmed, and stay that way while they slide — only their position changes.
tests.key: slotSamples › holds outer slots at the capped tilt and dimming

tests.description: The whole point of the rewrite: as the rail travels one card, the arriving neighbour must land looking exactly like the centre card did — upright, undimmed, not dropped. The earlier version snapped here instead.
tests.key: slotSamples › lands an arriving neighbour in the exact centre pose

tests.description: At rest the centre slot is dead centre, upright, undimmed and not dropped — this is the frame the user actually chooses from.
tests.key: slotSamples › puts the centre slot at rest upright, centred and at full opacity

tests.description: Each property is sampled at rail-left, rest, rail-right.
tests.key: slotSamples › returns three samples for every property

tests.description: Symmetrically, the departing centre card must land exactly where the side card was, so resetting the rail to the new index is invisible.
tests.key: slotSamples › sends the departing centre card into the exact side pose

tests.description: The two languages must actually differ, or a key was copy-pasted.
tests.key: t › differs between languages for translated copy

tests.description: Every key must exist in both languages — a missing one would render `undefined` on screen rather than falling back.
tests.key: t › has a non-empty string for every key in both languages

tests.description: The same key resolves to the English copy.
tests.key: t › returns the English string for the same key

tests.description: Each language returns its own copy.
tests.key: t › returns the Spanish string for a key

tests.description: Local calendar day, not UTC — the day turns over at the user's midnight.
tests.key: toDateKey › formats a date as YYYY-MM-DD

tests.description: Just before midnight is still today, in local time.
tests.key: toDateKey › uses the local day, not UTC

tests.description: Single-digit months and days are zero-padded so keys sort lexically.
tests.key: toDateKey › zero-pads single-digit months and days

tests.description: The scenario seam: a seeded day wins, so a capture taken in August still renders the day it was authored for.
tests.key: today › honours a seeded override

tests.description: Without a seeded override it reads the clock.
tests.key: today › returns the real day when nothing is seeded

tests.description: The screen offers "ver cartas anteriores" only when there is history.
tests.key: useDailyCard › counts past cards

tests.description: Revealing an open card would rewrite storage for nothing.
tests.key: useDailyCard › does not write again when the card is already revealed

tests.description: Once loaded the card and its day are available.
tests.key: useDailyCard › exposes the loaded card and its date

tests.description: A load that resolves after the screen closes must not set state.
tests.key: useDailyCard › ignores a load that lands after unmount

tests.description: Revealing before the load lands must not throw or write a draw that does not exist yet.
tests.key: useDailyCard › ignores a reveal that arrives before the card has loaded

tests.description: A card already opened earlier today stays open — the reveal is once a day, not once a visit.
tests.key: useDailyCard › reports an already-revealed card as revealed

tests.description: Tapping turns the card immediately and records it.
tests.key: useDailyCard › reveals optimistically and persists the reveal

tests.description: Storage is async, so the first frame has nothing yet — the screen must be able to render that state rather than blanking.
tests.key: useDailyCard › starts empty before storage answers

tests.description: Leaving the screen mid-turn must not leave a timer to fire into an unmounted component.
tests.key: useTurnUp › cancels a pending turn when unmounted

tests.description: And still face-down partway through the delay.
tests.key: useTurnUp › has not turned before the delay has elapsed

tests.description: The card must be on screen face-down first — turning immediately would skip the flip the whole reveal is built around.
tests.key: useTurnUp › is still face-down in the moment the card arrives

tests.description: "Elegir otra carta" clears the card; the next one has to start face-down again or the spread would reopen onto an already-revealed card.
tests.key: useTurnUp › returns to face-down when the card is cleared

tests.description: With no card chosen there is nothing to turn.
tests.key: useTurnUp › stays face-down when there is no card

tests.description: Choosing a different card re-runs the whole turn, rather than inheriting the previous card's face-up state.
tests.key: useTurnUp › turns again for a newly chosen card

tests.description: Then it turns on its own, with no further interaction.
tests.key: useTurnUp › turns face-up once the delay has elapsed
